title: Circumferential Stress (Longitudinal Joints) — Inside Radius

variables.S: maximum allowable stress value (MPa) per Table UCS-23 at design temperature

applicability: When t does not exceed one-half of the inside radius, or P does not exceed 0.385 × S × E
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-27(c)(1)

title: Longitudinal Stress (Circumferential Joints) — Inside Radius

variables.E: joint efficiency for circumferential joint per Table UW-12

applicability: When t does not exceed one-half of the inside radius, or P does not exceed 1.25 × S × E
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-27(c)(2)

title: Circumferential Stress — Outside Radius
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-27(c)(2)

title: Spherical Shells
applicability: When t does not exceed 0.356R, or P does not exceed 0.665 × S × E
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-27(d)

title: Ellipsoidal Heads — Pressure on Concave Side

variables.t: minimum required thickness of head after forming (mm)
variables.D: inside diameter of the head skirt (mm)
variables.E: lowest joint efficiency per Table UW-12 of any Category A joint in the head

applicability: For 2:1 semi-ellipsoidal heads where ts/L ≥ 0.002. An acceptable approximation of a 2:1 ellipsoidal head is one with a knuckle radius of 0.17D and a spherical radius of 0.90D.
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-32(c)

title: Torispherical Heads — Pressure on Concave Side

variables.t: minimum required thickness of head after forming (mm)
variables.E: lowest joint efficiency per Table UW-12 of any Category A joint in the head

applicability: For torispherical heads where ts/L ≥ 0.002 and the knuckle radius is 6% of the inside crown radius
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-32(d)

title: Hemispherical Heads — Pressure on Concave Side

variables.t: minimum required thickness of head after forming (mm)

applicability: When t does not exceed 0.356L, or P does not exceed 0.665 × S × E
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-32(e)

title: Unstayed Flat Heads and Covers — Circular

variables.t: minimum required thickness of flat head or cover (mm)
variables.d: diameter or short span, measured per UG-34(d) (mm)
variables.C: factor depending on the method of attachment, from UG-34 Table
variables.E: joint efficiency per Table UW-12 (for welded heads)

applicability: For circular flat heads and covers attached by various methods per UG-34(c)(2)
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-34(c)(2)

title: Nozzle Reinforcement — Area Replacement Method

variables.A_required: total cross-sectional area of reinforcement required (mm²)
variables.d: finished diameter of the opening in its corroded condition (mm)
variables.tr: required thickness of the shell or head, computed by the applicable formula (mm)
variables.F: correction factor, normally 1.0 for nozzles on the axis

applicability: Openings in shells and heads shall be reinforced when the area replacement method demonstrates that available reinforcement area ≥ required area. Available area comes from excess shell thickness, nozzle wall thickness, and added pads per UG-40.
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-37

title: Maximum Allowable Working Pressure — Cylindrical Shell (Inside Radius)

variables.S: maximum allowable stress value at design temperature (MPa)

applicability: Back-calculation of MAWP from UG-27(c)(1) formula, using corroded thickness
source: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1, UG-27(c)(1) rearranged
